channel.title: The Franchise Profit Playbook — Blog
channel.description: Operational insights for franchise operators from Mary Pillow Thompson.

item.title: The $2-an-Hour Raise That Cut One Operator's Labor Costs by 2%
item.description: One operator raised pay $2 an hour. Labor costs went down 2%. Here's the shift-design mechanism behind it — and why most operators never see it.

item.title: The Store You Think You're Running Isn't the One Your Team Is Experiencing
item.description: Most operators manage by reviewing results. The ones who consistently outperform see the business clearly while it's still unfolding.

item.title: 3 Conditions Every High-Performing Shift Has (Most Stores Have Zero)
item.description: Two shifts. Same store. Same team. One hits the number easily. The other struggles all day. The difference comes down to three conditions.

item.title: The 3 Things That DON'T Increase Sales — And the 5 Things That DO
item.description: Operators try a lot of things to move revenue. Most don't work. Here's what the data — and real operational experience — actually shows.

item.title: Why Your $85 SPLH Target Is Mathematically Impossible — And What to Use Instead
item.description: A flat SPLH target that doesn't vary by daypart isn't a real operational tool. Here's how to build one that actually is.

item.title: You Don't Need Better People. You Need a Better Game.
item.description: Every operator has said it. "If I could just find better people…" It's one of the most common refrains in franchise operations — and it's almost never the real problem.

item.title: Your Employees Are Either Your Revenue Engine or Your Biggest Missed Opportunity
item.description: Your frontline team is not a cost center. They are the primary mechanism through which revenue is generated — or lost.

item.title: You're Not Ready to Open a Second Store
item.description: The question isn't whether you can afford a second location. It's whether your first one can run without you.

item.title: Consistency
item.description: Consistency is not a personality trait you hire for. It is an output of deliberate design — and most locations are missing the systems that produce it.

item.title: The Operating System Behind Every Winning Shift
item.description: Great shifts don't happen by accident. They run on a three-part operating system — and you can install it in any location.

item.title: Where the Day Is Actually Won
item.description: Most operators think the day is won in the rush. It isn't. The day is won in the thirty minutes before the first customer walks in.

item.title: The Store You Think You're Running Isn't the One Your Team Is Experiencing
item.description: There is a gap between the store you see as the owner and the store your team actually experiences. That gap is costing you more than you know.

item.title: Why Some Shifts Produce $500 More Than Others
item.description: Same store. Same menu. Same team size. $500 difference. It comes down to three conditions — and none of them is customer count.

item.title: The Behavior Lever That Actually Moves Volume
item.description: Most operators reach for promotions or motivation speeches when sales are flat. The lever that actually works is simpler — and it starts with information.

item.title: Why Labor % Is Lying to You
item.description: Labor percentage is the most commonly watched number in franchise operations. And it's leading a lot of operators in exactly the wrong direction.
